blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Pulmonary Vascular Development?
ทารกแรกเกิดทุกคนต้องผ่าน pulmonary vascular transition — การลด PVR จาก fetal pattern (16% CO ไปปอด) → postnatal pattern (100% RV output ไปปอด) ภายในนาที. เมื่อ transition ล้มเหลว เกิด PPHN ซึ่งพบใน 10% ของ term/preterm ที่มี respiratory failure, mortality 7–9%. ผลลัพธ์เพิ่มเติม: (1) 25% survivors มี significant NDD; 6–10% มี sensorineural hearing loss; (2) CDH = most common ECMO indication in neonates; (3) BPD-PH: 16–25% ใน BPD, สูงถึง 40% ใน severe BPD — เป็น hidden burden ใน NICU; (4) iNO = only FDA-approved selective pulmonary vasodilator — ลด ECMO แต่ไม่ลด mortality; (5) เข้าใจ NO-cGMP / PGI2-cAMP / ET-1 pathways → รู้ mechanism ของยาทุกตัวที่ใช้ใน NICU. บท 73 นี้คือ foundation ที่อธิบาย PPHN ทั้งระบบ.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย structural development ของ pulmonary vasculature: vasculogenesis vs angiogenesis, timeline (5 wk → postnatal), lung stage development
ระบุ molecular regulators ของ vascular growth: HIF-1α/2α, VEGF/VEGFR, eNOS, NO, Foxf1 — และผลของ disruption ในแต่ละ pathway
อธิบาย fetal pulmonary vascular tone: สาเหตุของ high PVR (low O2, ↓ vasodilators, ↑ vasoconstrictors: ET-1, serotonin, RhoA/ROCK) พร้อมตัวเลขสำคัญ
อธิบาย pulmonary vascular transition at birth: triggers (lung inflation + O2), vasoactive mediators (NO, PGI2), structural changes ของ small arteries
จำแนก PPHN 3 categories (parenchymal/idiopathic/hypoplastic) และ risk factors สำคัญ (SSRI, Down syndrome, elective CS, maternal diabetes)
ระบุ genetic associations ใน PPHN: CRHR1, urea cycle enzymes (CPS-1), TBX4, FOXF1 (ACD) — และ approach ต่อ whole genome sequencing
อธิบาย CDH: pulmonary hypoplasia mechanism, LV hypoplasia, role ของ milrinone ใน LV dysfunction, chronic PH + ET-1
อธิบาย Alveolar Capillary Dysplasia: presentation, pathology, FOXF1 variants, prognosis, ไม่ตอบสนองต่อ ECMO
อธิบาย BPD-PH: incidence, mechanism (impaired angiogenesis, vascular pruning), pulmonary vein stenosis, surveillance
ระบุ management PPHN ตาม AHA/ATS guidelines: general care, oxygen target, lung recruitment, pH >7.3, iNO, sildenafil, prostanoids, milrinone, bosentan, ECMO
อธิบาย neurodevelopmental outcomes ของ PPHN survivors: %, type ของ disability, follow-up ที่ต้องทำ
1. Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Development — Structure
1.1 Developmental Stages and Timeline
Week 5 GA: endodermal lung buds arise from ventral foregut
Week 8: spiral aortopulmonary septum divides truncus arteriosus → aorta + pulmonary trunks; PA arises from 6th branchial arch artery
Week 20: pre-acinar vascular branching pattern complete in human lung; intra-acinar arteries form later (fetal life + postnatal alveolar phase)
Week 32–36: septation + alveolarization begins; vascular growth tightly coupled to airway branching
In fetal lambs (last trimester): lung weight ↑ 4x, but number of small blood vessels ↑ 40x — dramatic ↑ surface area prepares for 10x ↑ blood flow at birth
Final stage: pulmonary capillaries surround thinning alveolar walls → alveolar + capillary surface area for gas exchange at birth
1.2 Vasculogenesis vs Angiogenesis
Vasculogenesis: de novo formation — endothelial progenitor cells (angioblasts) migrate, differentiate, form vascular tubes → arteries/veins/lymphatics depending on local growth factors in mesenchyme
Angiogenesis: budding, sprouting, branching from existing vessels. Both processes occur early; may give rise to heterogeneous cell populations
Additionally: vascular fusion connects angiogenic + vasculogenic vessels → allows expansion of vascular network
1.3 Molecular Regulators of Vascular Growth

HIF → VEGF → eNOS/NO — Master Cascade ของ Fetal Vascular Growth
HIFs (Hypoxia-Inducible Factors): heterodimers of O2-sensitive α-subunit (HIF-1α, HIF-2α) + constitutive β-subunit. 'Master regulators' ในสภาวะ hypoxic intrauterine. HIF-1α/2α deletion → embryonic lethality; HIF-1α KO in SMC → PH in mice. VEGF: expressed in airway epithelium + endothelium + SMC; transcribed by HIF; acts via VEGFR-1 and VEGFR-2. VEGF inhibition/knockout → lung hypoplasia similar to BPD (animal); decreased pulmonary VEGF/VEGFR-1 mRNA in infants dying with BPD (Bhatt Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2001). eNOS: VEGF-induced lung angiogenesis partly mediated by NO; eNOS mRNA ↑ with advancing gestation (rats, sheep). eNOS-KO mice: striking abnormalities of vascularization; susceptible to failed vascular growth under mild hypoxia. FOXF1: Forkhead Box transcription factor; haploinsufficiency in 40% of alveolar capillary dysplasia infants; newborn mice with low Foxf1 → die with vascular + alveolar defects.
2. Mediators of Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Tone
2.1 Why High PVR Is Normal in the Fetus

ใน fetal lamb: pulmonary arterial PO2 ≈ 18 mmHg, SaO2 ≈ 50%; PVR สูง → เลือดแค่ 16% ของ combined ventricular output ไปปอด; ส่วนใหญ่ bypass ผ่าน foramen ovale + ductus arteriosus → descending aorta → oxygenated ที่ placenta → กลับมา via umbilical vein (PO2 ≈ 32–35 mmHg)

High PVR maintained by: (1) low O2 tension; (2) ↓ vasodilators (PGI2, NO); (3) ↑ vasoconstrictors; (4) myogenic response: vasculature responds to vasodilatory stimuli with active vasoconstriction (↑ with gestation)
2.2 Vasoconstrictors

ET-1, Serotonin, RhoA/ROCK — Fetal Vasoconstrictors
Endothelin-1 (ET-1): produced by vascular endothelium. ET-A receptor on SMC → vasoconstriction (↑ intracellular Ca²⁺). ET-B receptor on endothelium → NO release + vasodilation. PreproET-1 mRNA present early in fetal lung; high ET-1 in umbilical cord blood. ET-1 primarily acts as pulmonary vasoconstrictor in fetus. Serotonin (5-HT): infusion of 5-HT in fetal lambs ↑ PVR; ketanserin (5-HT2A antagonist) ↓ fetal PVR. SSRIs (sertraline, fluoxetine) → potent, sustained ↑ PVR via 5-HT2A + Rho kinase activation. RhoA/ROCK: downstream signaling; inactivates myosin light chain phosphatase → ↑ Ca²⁺ sensitivity → vasoconstriction; activated by hypoxia, ductal constriction, ET-1; ROCK inhibition dilates perinatal circulation independent of NO. Lipid mediators: thromboxane, LTC4/D4, platelet-activating factor — proposed vasoconstrictors (none proven as central regulator). Low O2 + low NOS: hypoxia ↓ NOS + prostacyclin activity. Maternal hyperoxia: NO EFFECT on blood flow <26 wk GA; significant ↑ at 31–36 wk — developmentally regulated.
2.3 NO-cGMP Pathway — Late Fetal Emergence
As gestation progresses, NO-cGMP becomes central; PGs play lesser role vs NO
sGC (soluble guanylate cyclase): mRNA levels low in 2nd trimester → markedly ↑ at end of 3rd trimester; NO binds sGC → ↑ cGMP → SMC relaxation
PDE5: cGMP-specific phosphodiesterase; ↑ expression + activity during late gestation → tightly regulates cGMP levels; critical role in perinatal vasoregulation

STRIDER trial (international): sildenafil (PDE5 inhibitor) during pregnancy for severe IUGR → 12% PPHN in sildenafil arm (Dutch cohort) → SUSPENDED; UK/NZ-Australia arm: no benefit. PDE5 inhibition antenatally = harmful in severe IUGR context
Chronic VEGFR inhibition → ↓ eNOS → PH in late gestation fetus (Grover 2003): confirms VEGF-eNOS-NO axis critical for fetal vascular development
eNOS inhibition in fetal lambs ↑ basal PVR from 0.75 gestation (112 days): endogenous NOS contributes to vasoregulation during late gestation
3. Pulmonary Vascular Transition at Birth
3.1 The Event — PVR Drop
At birth: 8–10x ↑ pulmonary blood flow; ↑ pulmonary venous return → ↑ left atrial pressure → functional closure of foramen ovale
Systemic vascular resistance ↑ (cord clamping removes low-resistance placental bed)
Largest PVR drop at/shortly after birth; continues over first several months → reaches adult low-PVR pattern
PVR ↓ → blood through PDA reverses + DA functionally closes → separates pulmonary + systemic circulations
3.2 Primary Triggers

Lung inflation with gas (independently ↓ PVR): mechanical distension → ↑ external diameter of non-muscular arteries; endothelial cells flatten; 'spreading' of SMC → ↑ lumen diameter → ↓ resistance (facilitated by paucity of interstitial connective tissue for greater plasticity)
↑ Oxygen tension (independently ↓ PVR): develops sensitivity in 3rd trimester (after 26 wk); largest effect when ventilation + oxygenation occur simultaneously
↑ Shear stress: initial ↑ blood flow → shear stress in pulmonary vasculature → further vasodilation via K⁺ channels + NOS activation
Postmortem data: non-muscular arteries recruited rapidly; 24-h increase in arteries filling with injection material = ↑ precapillary arteries entering circulation
3.3 Vasoactive Mediators at Transition

NO-cGMP, PGI2-cAMP, และ PDE5 — ตัวเล่นหลักในการ transition
Nitric oxide (NO) — central mediator: stimulates sGC → ↑ cGMP → ↓ myosin light chain phosphorylation → SMC relaxation. eNOS expression + sGC ↑ late gestation (preparing lung for transition). Acute/chronic NOS inhibition in fetal lambs → PH after delivery. PDE5 expression peaks at birth: tightly regulates cGMP at this critical moment. Prostacyclin (PGI2): 2nd central vasodilator; upregulated by ventilation. COX-1 ↑ in late gestation → ↑ PGI2. PGI2 stimulates adenylate cyclase → ↑ cAMP → vasorelaxation. PGI2 effect modest vs NO; COX pharmacologic inhibition does NOT prevent PVR drop at birth — PGI2 involved but not absolutely required. PDE3A: breaks down cAMP; milrinone inhibits PDE3 in cardiomyocytes + vascular SMC.

Pulmonary veins: reactive vessels (not just passive conduits); NO ↑ cGMP + relaxation MORE in veins than arteries (O2-dependent); ET-1 + thromboxane act primarily on pulmonary veins → ↑ microvascular pressure → pulmonary edema
4. Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn (PPHN)
4.1 Definition and Scope
PPHN: failure of normal pulmonary vascular adaptation at birth → ↑ PVR + right-to-left extrapulmonary shunting → severe hypoxemia
Frequency: ~10% of term + preterm infants with respiratory failure; significant risk of death, pulmonary morbidity, NDD
2018 Nice Classification (6th World Symposium): PPHN = separate subcategory of Group 1 (PAH); CDH/BPD/ACD = Group 3 (PH due to lung disease/hypoxia)
4.2 Three Categories of PPHN

rows: Pulmonary vasoconstriction from ↓ alveolar O2; always has some vascular remodeling
MAS, RDS, pneumonia, sepsis — most common; 80–90% of PPHN cases
Normal parenchyma; antenatal vascular remodeling; SM hyperplasia extending to intra-acinar level
Idiopathic PPHN; SSRI-exposed; antenatal ductal constriction — 10–20% of cases
Reduced cross-sectional area of vascular bed + structural remodeling; altered reactivity + LV dysfunction
CDH — most severe; also pulmonary hypoplasia

blocks.text: 4.3 Risk Factors — Antenatal

SSRI ใน Pregnancy กับ PPHN — Evidence สรุป
Animal data: fluoxetine → PH + hypoxia + ↑ mortality in rat pups (Fornaro Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2007). Mechanism: SSRIs → ductal constriction (dose-dependent, confirmed in mouse model) → idiopathic PPHN pattern. Human epidemiology: Chambers NEJM 2006: SSRI in late pregnancy → ↑ PPHN (OR ~6x). Kieler BMJ 2012 (Nordic, 5 countries): confirmed ↑ PPHN risk. But absolute risk small when controlling for maternal depression. Meta-analysis 11 studies, 156,978 pregnancies (Masarwa Am J Obstet Gynecol 2019): SSRIs → 2x ↑ PPHN risk; sertraline had lowest probability risk of all antidepressants. FDA: continue treatment as clinically appropriate — weigh PPHN risk vs untreated depression risk. SSRI-exposed infants: more likely idiopathic PPHN without lung disease (Swedish registry). Neonatal mortality NOT significantly higher in SSRI-exposed PPHN (3.4% vs 8.3%, NS).

NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen, naproxen): early reports strong association; large multicenter study (Van Marter Pediatrics 2013): NO significant association between non-aspirin NSAIDs or ibuprofen in 3rd trimester and PPHN risk
Elective cesarean section: delays ↓ pulmonary arterial pressure; ↑ PPHN risk; delivery before 39 wk likely amplifies effect; infants with PPHN more likely to have been born by CS
Maternal factors: diabetes, asthma, Black/Asian race, high BMI → ↑ PPHN risk (Hernandez-Diaz Pediatrics 2007; Wilson Am J Perinatol 2011)

Delayed cord clamping: in preterm lambs: DCC 3–4 min until ventilation established → ↑ PV return + LV filling before cessation of UV return; DCC in CDH lambs → improved cardiopulmonary hemodynamics + enhanced PVR drop (Kashyap Arch Dis Child 2020)

Perinatal asphyxia: PPHN complicates 25% of perinatal asphyxia; acute asphyxia → reversible vasoconstriction; chronic → vascular remodeling. Moderate therapeutic hypothermia (33.5°C): does NOT significantly ↑ PPHN incidence; but PPHN ↑ mortality in HIE (Lakshminrusimha J Pediatr 2018). Deep hypothermia (32°C): +30% mean PA pressure in lambs
4.4 Genetics of PPHN
Rarely familial; few genetic risk factors identified
Genotype analysis 88 neonates (Byers Pediatr Res 2012): no differences in adult PAH genes (BMPR2, NOS). CRHR1 (corticotropin-releasing hormone receptor-1) variants + ↑ 17-hydroxyprogesterone → PPHN

Urea cycle enzyme genes (Kaluarachchi Pediatr Res 2018): polymorphisms in 6 UCA genes including CPS-1 (carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase-1); associated with low plasma citrulline + arginine — endothelial NO synthesis substrate

TBX4 (T-box transcription factor 4): variants reported in infants with prolonged pulmonary hypertension (Galambos Eur Respir J 2019); role not fully understood; whole genome sequencing should be considered in atypical or prolonged PPHN

Down syndrome (trisomy 21): 10–100x ↑ incidence of PPHN without cardiac disease; Dutch cohort: PPHN in 5.2% of T21 infants (Bush Cardiol Ther 2022); ↑ in ECMO registry; ↓ survival to discharge vs general population. LV diastolic impairment in T21 → independently associated with PH in first week (Smith J Pediatr 2022)
4.5 Molecular Pathophysiology
Decreased eNOS expression + activity: documented in animal PPHN models; ↓ eNOS in umbilical venous endothelial cells from MAS infants who develop PPHN
eNOS uncoupling: ↓ NO synthesis + ↑ reactive oxygen species (superoxide) → ↓ cGMP + vasoconstriction + vascular remodeling
PDE5 elevation: ↑ in PPHN vascular SMC; further ↑ after mechanical ventilation + O2 therapy → ↓↓ cGMP levels; drives interest in PDE5 inhibitors (sildenafil)

ET-1 elevation: circulating ET-1 ↑ in PPHN lambs + neonates; ET-A pathway shifted (vasoconstrictor); ET-1 → ↑ ROS (superoxide, H2O2) → additional vasoconstriction; ET-1 correlates with worse outcomes + severity of PH in CDH (Keller Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2010)

Oxidant stress: ↑ superoxide + H2O2 in SMC + adventitia of PA in animal PPHN models; hyperoxia: ↓ eNOS expression + ↓ sGC activity + ↑ PDE5 → ↓ cGMP → worse PH; NADPH oxidase (prooxidant enzyme) implicated
5. Special Disorders of Pulmonary Vascular Development
5.1 Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia (CDH)

CDH — Pulmonary Vascular Pathology
Epidemiology: 1:2500–3000 pregnancies (including prenatal diagnosis); 8% of all major congenital anomalies; most common ECMO indication in neonates. Pathology: arrest in airway branching → ↓ lung volume + ↓ alveolarization BOTH lungs; ↓ PA branching → ↓ cross-sectional vascular area; thickened media + adventitia; SM hyperplasia to acinar arterioles. Key: low pulmonary blood flow alone sufficient to cause lung hypoplasia (animal data). After birth: PVR often suprasystemic → right-to-left shunting across FO + DA + profound hypoxemia. LV hypoplasia: LV, LA, IVS hypoplastic in CDH infants who die (Siebert J Pediatr Surg 1984) — because low fetal PBF + compression by hypertensive RV. LV dysfunction → ↑ LA pressure → ↑ PV pressure → ↓ response to iNO in first few days. Some have severe LV dysfunction with dependence on RV for systemic perfusion → consider milrinone (targets LV dysfunction) ± maintain PDA patency (Kumar Pediatr Surg Int 2018).
5.2 Alveolar Capillary Dysplasia (ACD)
Rare interstitial lung disease → severe PH + hypoxemia early in life; etiology = genetic defect or early antenatal insult preventing normal pulmonary capillary bed development

Pathology: (1) remodeling of pulmonary arterioles; (2) simplification of alveolar architecture; (3) 'misaligned pulmonary veins' — bronchopulmonary anastomoses linking systemic + pulmonary circulations, bypassing alveolar capillary bed; PA + vein share adventitial sheath
Prognosis: generally fatal even with ECMO; limited case reports of survival after lung transplant
Associated anomalies in >50%: genitourinary, cardiovascular, GI

Genetics: FOXF1 mutations/deletions in 40%+ of infants (Stankiewicz Am J Hum Genet 2009; Jourdan-Voyen Arch Dis Child 2020); >70 FOXF1 variants + >60 CNV deletions involving gene + upstream regulatory region; 10% familial
Classic presentation: neonatal period; but late presentation (months of life) reported — timely lung biopsy may help avoid futile treatment if genetic analysis delayed
Complete postmortem evaluation should be performed for all newborns dying of unexplained or prolonged PH
5.3 Genetic Surfactant Deficiency + PPHN
SP-B deficiency (most common): early presentation; ground-glass opacities; progressive respiratory failure; early death. Most common mutation: codon 121 of SP-B gene
SP-C deficiency: occurs but PPHN not a known association
ABCA3 (ATP-binding cassette transporter 3) mutations: severe neonatal lung disease; surfactant deficiency symptoms; lethal cause of PPHN reported
5.4 Pulmonary Hypertension in Premature Infants

BPD-PH — Incidence, Mechanism, Pulmonary Vein Stenosis
Early PPHN in extreme prematurity: 10x higher rate than term; particularly with oligohydramnios. Japan Neonatal Research Network: overall PPHN incidence 8.1%, highest in most premature (Lakshminrusimha J Pediatr 2020). Early echo signs (7 days): ventricular septal wall flattening + RV dilation in 40% of ELBW → predicted BPD + late PH (Mourani Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2015). BPD-PH: incidence 16–25% in BPD; up to 40% in severe BPD (Mourani Clin Perinatol 2015). Strongly associated with: growth restriction, placental dysfunction, preeclampsia, oligohydramnios, fetal inflammation. Also: ROP, NEC → probable involvement of hypoxia, hyperoxia, inflammation. Morphology: ↓ angiogenesis, vascular pruning, ↓ alveolar-capillary surface area + ↑ vasoconstrictor responses to hypoxia. Pulmonary vein stenosis (PVS): complication of severe BPD; left-sided veins (especially left upper) most affected. Median diagnosis 6 months. Progressive; mortality 30–50% in first 2 years. Not well-understood mechanism; postnatally acquired (lack of twin concordance + NEC association).
6. Clinical Diagnosis of PPHN
6.1 Clinical Presentation
Respiratory distress + labile oxygenation in term/preterm infants
Differential cyanosis: higher SpO2 in right upper extremity vs lower extremity — right-to-left shunting at ductus arteriosus (pre-ductal vs post-ductal)
Profound hypoxemia despite O2 + mechanical ventilation
6.2 Echocardiography — Mandatory
ECHO is mandatory: establish accurate PPHN diagnosis; identify extrapulmonary shunting; MUST rule out CHD (especially total anomalous pulmonary venous return)
Findings confirming PPHN: bidirectional or predominantly R→L shunting across FO or DA; IVS flattening/left deviation; ↑ tricuspid regurgitant velocity; ↑ RV dilation
Key determination: is LV dysfunction present? LV dysfunction → pulmonary venous hypertension → pulmonary vasodilator would WORSEN the situation (iNO not first-line in pure LV failure)
OI (Oxygenation Index): OI = (MAP × FiO2 × 100) / PaO2; OI >25 = indication for iNO; OI >40 = consider ECMO referral
BNP: ↑ with elevated RV pressure but insufficient sensitivity/specificity for diagnosis/monitoring
7. Management of PPHN — General Care

AHA/ATS Management Guidelines for PPHN (Abman Circulation 2015)
Class I, LOE A: (1) iNO indicated to ↓ need for ECMO in term/near-term infants with PPHN or hypoxemic RF with OI >25. (2) Lung recruitment strategies to improve iNO efficacy + should be performed in PPHN with parenchymal lung disease. (3) ECMO indicated for term/near-term neonates with severe PH or hypoxemia refractory to iNO + optimization of respiratory + cardiac function. Class IIa, LOE B: (4) Evaluate for ACD + genetic surfactant diseases in severe PPHN failing vasodilator + lung recruitment + ECMO. (5) Sildenafil reasonable adjunctive therapy for PPHN refractory to iNO (OI >25). (8) iNO beneficial for preterm with severe hypoxemia primarily from PPHN rather than parenchymal disease, especially if oligo + PPROM. Class IIb: (6) Inhaled prostacyclin analogues may be considered as adjunctive for PPHN refractory to iNO (OI >25). (7) IV milrinone reasonable in PPHN + LV dysfunction signs.
7.1 General Support Principles
Maintain normal temperature (except therapeutic hypothermia for HIE), electrolytes (especially calcium), glucose, intravascular volume
BP target: maintain normal for age via volume + cardiotonic therapy; primary goal = optimize LV + RV function + systemic O2 transport
Avoid supraphysiologic BP solely to drive L→R shunt at PDA: transient O2 improvement but extreme caution; must simultaneously ↓ PVR

Avoid dopamine as vasopressor: ↑ systemic resistance → may compromise LV performance + worsen hemodynamics (McNamara J Pediatr 2022). Milrinone (inodilator) preferred: ↑ LV function + ↓ PV hypertension → ↑ PBF + O2 in refractory PPHN
7.2 Oxygen and Lung Recruitment
Avoid alveolar hypoxia + hypoxemia: both are pulmonary vasoconstrictors

O2 vasodilatory effect plateaus at ~60% O2 or PaO2 ~60 mmHg (Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2009): hyperoxia beyond this: ↑ oxidant stress + ↑ pulmonary vascular contractility + ↓ vasodilator response to NO
Therapeutic hypothermia + PPHN: O2 dissociation curve shifts LEFT → PaO2 at lower saturations; factor in when monitoring SpO2; correct blood gases to patient's actual body temperature
Lung recruitment: target 9–10 ribs expansion; HFV + surfactant for severe parenchymal disease (RDS/MAS)

Surfactant in PPHN: ✓ for MAS + sepsis + pneumonia (↓ CS, ↓ air leak, ↓ ECMO; Lotze J Pediatr 1998). ✗ for idiopathic PH without parenchymal disease (risk overdistension/airway obstruction). ✗ for CDH (does NOT benefit; Van Meurs J Pediatr 2004)
7.3 pH Management
pH goal: >7.3; acidosis is a pulmonary vasoconstrictor especially with hypoxia (Rudolph J Clin Invest 1966)

Alkalosis via hyperventilation or NaHCO3: AVOID — historically used before iNO for transient PaO2 ↑; but: alkalosis does NOT improve survival; worsens PVR/reactivity/permeability edema + causes cerebral vasoconstriction → ↓ CBF → worse neurodevelopmental outcomes
8. Pulmonary Vasodilator Therapy
8.1 Inhaled Nitric Oxide (iNO)

iNO — Only FDA-Approved Selective Pulmonary Vasodilator
Mechanism: inhaled NO → ↑ cGMP in pulmonary SMC → relaxation; selective (inhaled → vasodilates only ventilated lung segments → ↓ V/Q mismatch). Dose: 20 ppm (doses >20 ppm: no benefit + ↑ methemoglobinemia risk). Indication: OI >25 in term/near-term. iNO acutely ↑ oxygenation + ↓ need for ECMO (Class I, LOE A). But iNO has NOT reduced mortality or LOS in any clinical trial. Optimal window: OI 15–25 — early iNO does NOT improve outcomes (mortality, ECMO, CLD, NDD). Delaying until OI >40 may ↑ time on O2 (Gonzalez J Perinatol 2010). Preterm use: early acute PPHN especially after PPROM/oligohydramnios → marked O2 ↑ (Kinsella J Pediatr 2016; Class IIa LOE B). Weaning: usually wean + DC within 5 days. 'Rebound PH' on abrupt D/C (even if no initial response) → wean to 1 ppm before stopping.
8.2 Sildenafil (PDE5 Inhibitor)
Up to 40% of infants do not respond or sustain response to iNO; PDE5 ↑ in PPHN → PDE5 inhibitors as adjunctive agents
Pilot studies (enteral or IV, alone or + iNO): dramatic O2 improvement + survival in small series; systemic hypotension most common adverse effect (especially with rapid IV loading dose)
PPHN RCT (Pierce J Pediatr 2021): multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled; IV sildenafil → NO benefit on treatment failure or time on iNO (incorporated standardized iNO weaning protocol)
Enteral sildenafil: successful long-term use in chronic PH from CDH + BPD; no RCT available. Additional trials underway for BPD/BPD-PH prevention (NCT02261883; NCT in progress)
Little data for other PDE5 inhibitors (tadalafil) in PPHN
8.3 Prostanoids
IV PGI2 (prostacyclin): used in adult/pediatric PH; rarely in neonates (concerns about systemic hypotension + V/Q mismatch in parenchymal disease)

Inhaled PGI2 (aerosolized): case series in iNO-refractory PPHN: transient pulmonary vasodilation + ↑ O2. Limitation: alkaline solution irritates airway; dose delivery imprecise (loss into nebulization circuit)

Iloprost + Treprostinil: more stable; specifically designed for intermittent nebulization. Treprostinil: used in chronic PH from CDH + BPD; can be given SC route; PPHN trial NCT02261883 has completed enrollment
8.4 Milrinone (PDE3 Inhibitor)
Mechanism: PDE3A inhibitor → ↑ cAMP in cardiomyocytes + vascular SMC → ↓ PVR + ↑ inotropy. PDE3A ↑ after birth + in response to iNO → counters vasodilatory response
Animal studies: milrinone ↓ PVR, additive with iNO, prevents rebound PH
Clinical: milrinone may ↑ pulmonary vasodilation + reverse LV diastolic dysfunction in CDH; CDH milrinone RCT underway (NCT02951130)
Class IIb: reasonable in PPHN + LV dysfunction signs
8.5 Bosentan (ET Receptor Antagonist)
ET-1 ↑ in PPHN + CDH; balance shifted to ET-A (vasoconstrictor) pathway
Bosentan: nonspecific ET-1 receptor blocker; FDA approved for adults + children >3 years with PAH
Single-center trial: bosentan ↑ O2 in iNO-naive PPHN population

FUTURE-4 multicenter RCT (Steinhorn J Pediatr 2016): bosentan as adjunctive to iNO → did NOT improve PPHN outcomes, time on iNO, or time to extubation — possibly due to inconsistent intestinal absorption
8.6 ECMO
ECMO is the only therapy PROVEN to reduce mortality for PPHN; costly, labor-intensive, adverse effects: ICH, ligation of right common carotid artery
Indication: OI >40 (infants failing iNO + optimization of respiratory + cardiac function); Class I, LOE A
Mortality: 7.6% with iNO + ECMO era (CA cohort, Steurer Pediatrics 2017)
9. Outcomes and Follow-Up
9.1 Short-Term Outcomes
Before ECMO era: mortality ≈ 40% severe hypoxemic RF; major neurologic disability in 25–60% survivors
After ECMO introduction + iNO: mortality ≈ 7–9%
Post-discharge (first year): >25% reactive airways disease; 15–20% significant feeding difficulty + poor weight gain (<5th percentile)
Supplemental O2 need ↓ substantially by 2 years; reactive airways disease + rehospitalization remains high through school age
9.2 Neurodevelopmental Outcomes
~25% of PPHN survivors: significant NDD at 12–24 months; rates similar in moderate vs severe disease; NO therapy (including iNO) shown to improve developmental outcomes
Sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL): 6–10%, continues to emerge through school age → serial screening mandatory
School-age cohort (Rosenberg J Perinatol 2010): 9% severe intellectual disability (FSIQ <70); 7% moderate intellectual disability (IQ 70–84)
Neuroimaging before discharge: recommended for all moderate-severe PPHN (OI >25), even without ECMO/hypothermia
Comprehensive screening before school entry: all PPHN survivors → screen for subtle deficits + learning disabilities
Preterm infants with/without BPD: evidence of subclinical pulmonary vascular disease + RH dysfunction persisting into adolescence + young adulthood (Abman Neonatology 2021)
10. Algorithm — PPHN Management Pathway

rows: Confirm PPHN; rule out CHD (esp. TAPVR); assess for LV dysfunction; determine shunt direction; calculate OI
Normal temp/glucose/Ca²⁺/BP; avoid alkalosis; pH >7.3; milrinone if LV dysfunction
Target 9–10 ribs; surfactant for parenchymal disease (MAS/RDS/pneumonia); NOT for idiopathic PPHN or CDH
High O2 for vasoconstriction + delivery; plateau at ~60% O2 / PaO2 ~60 mmHg — avoid excess hyperoxia; adjust for hypothermia (left-shifted ODC)
20 ppm inhaled NO; wean within 5 days if improving; wean to 1 ppm before stopping (rebound PH); avoid >20 ppm
Sildenafil (enteral; IV RCT showed no benefit); inhaled prostanoids (iloprost/treprostinil); milrinone (+ iNO for cAMP augmentation)
Transfer to ECMO center if not already there; OI >40 = threshold; consider evaluation for ACD/surfactant gene disorders if failing ECMO
Neuroimaging before discharge (OI >25); serial SNHL screening; NDD assessment before school entry

blocks.text: 11. NICU Practical — เตรียมรับ + จัดการ PPHN
11.1 Recognition at Bedside
Differential SpO2: right hand (pre-ductal) > foot (post-ductal) → R→L ductal shunting = PPHN until proven otherwise
Labile O2: SpO2 swings จาก 99% → 70% ใน few minutes = hallmark PPHN; distinguish จาก parenchymal disease ที่ SpO2 ต่ำแต่ stable
Calculate OI: OI = MAP × FiO2 × 100 / PaO2; OI >25 = iNO threshold; OI >40 = ECMO threshold. ต้องคำนวณ serial เพื่อ track trend
11.2 Before Starting iNO
Echo ก่อน: rule out TAPVR (จะ worse กับ iNO — ↑ PV pressure ใน obstructed veins); ประเมิน LV function
Lung recruitment ก่อน: iNO ไปเฉพาะที่ ventilated lung; ถ้า atelectasis → iNO ไม่ถึง target
Monitor methemoglobin: เริ่มหลัง 1–2h ของ iNO; target <5%; ถ้า >10% → ↓ dose; ถ้า >20% → stop iNO
11.3 CDH-Specific
ไม่ให้ surfactant: does NOT benefit CDH; may cause harm
ระวัง LV dysfunction: echo ดู LV size + function; ถ้า LV เล็ก + dysfunctional → milrinone; consider maintaining PDA patency as RV relief
Chronic PH ใน CDH: ET-1 ↑: ET-1 correlates with severity; อาจ need long-term oral sildenafil + treprostinil
11.4 PPHN ใน Down Syndrome
Expect in 5.2% of T21 infants (Dutch cohort); LV diastolic dysfunction → check echo carefully; ↑ representation ใน ECMO registry; ↓ survival vs general population
Screen early for PPHN ใน T21 + respiratory distress; lower threshold สำหรับ echo
11.5 Rebound PH ตอน Wean iNO
Mechanism: วาสคูลาร์ cell อาจ upregulate vasoconstrictor pathways ใน response to exogenous NO
Prevention: wean iNO slowly; wean to 1 ppm ก่อน discontinue; ถ้า rebound เกิด → restart iNO ที่ dose เดิม; consider adding sildenafil enteral ก่อน wean iNO
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Hyperoxia ≠ Better Vasodilation: Plateau ที่ FiO2 ~60%
Vasodilatory effect ของ O2 reach plateau ที่ ~60% FiO2 หรือ PaO2 ~60 mmHg (Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2009). เกินจาก threshold นี้: เพิ่ม oxidant stress + ↑ pulmonary vascular contractility + ↓ vasodilator response ต่อ NO. ดังนั้น: 100% FiO2 ตลอด — ไม่จำเป็น + อาจ harmful. Target PaO2 60–80 mmHg (ตาม AHA/ATS guidelines). ในผู้ป่วย therapeutic hypothermia: ODC shift ซ้าย → PaO2 สูงขึ้น ณ saturation เดิม — ต้อง correct blood gas to patient's temperature ก่อน interpret.

PEARL 2 — ECHO ก่อน iNO เสมอ: TAPVR จะ Worse
PPHN phenotype ที่หน้าตาเหมือนกัน (differential cyanosis, labile O2, OI >25) อาจเป็น TAPVR ก็ได้ โดยเฉพาะ obstructed TAPVR. iNO ใน TAPVR → ↑ pulmonary blood flow + ↑ pressure ใน obstructed pulmonary veins → flash pulmonary edema + cardiovascular collapse. ดังนั้น: ECHO ก่อน start iNO ทุกกรณี — confirm extrapulmonary shunting (FO/DA), LV size + function, สายพัน pulmonary veins. Rule out TAPVR ก่อนเสมอ.

PEARL 3 — Wean iNO ถึง 1 ppm ก่อน Stop: Rebound PH อาจ Fatal
Rebound PH เกิดได้แม้ในรายที่ iNO ไม่ produce sustained improvement ตอนแรก. Mechanism: possible upregulation ของ vasoconstrictor pathways ใน response to exogenous NO. วิธีป้องกัน: wean iNO ช้า ๆ (ลดทีละ 1–2 ppm ทุก 2–4h); wean ถึง 1 ppm → hold → observe 30–60 min → discontinue. ถ้าเกิด rebound: restart iNO ที่ dose ก่อนหน้า; consider adding enteral sildenafil ก่อนทำ second attempt wean.

PEARL 4 — PPHN Survivors: 25% NDD + 6–10% SNHL → Follow-Up ครบระบบ
iNO ลด ECMO แต่ไม่ลด neurodevelopmental outcomes. 25% มี significant NDD; 6–10% มี SNHL ที่อาจปรากฏช้า; 9% มี severe intellectual disability (FSIQ <70). ดังนั้น: (1) neuroimaging ก่อน DC ทุก moderate-severe PPHN (OI >25 แม้ไม่ได้ ECMO/cooling); (2) serial SNHL screening (newborn hearing screen ไม่พอ — ต้อง serial ถึง school age); (3) comprehensive NDD assessment ก่อนเข้าโรงเรียน. อย่าบอก parents ว่า 'ตอนนี้ปกติ' โดยไม่มี proper follow-up plan.

PEARL 5 — Down Syndrome + PPHN: Expect + Prepare
T21 มี PPHN 10–100x มากกว่า general population; 5.2% ของ T21 infants (Dutch cohort). Mechanism สำคัญ: LV diastolic impairment → ↑ LA pressure → ↑ PV pressure → PH from venous side. ดังนั้น: (1) Lower threshold สำหรับ echo ใน T21 ที่มี respiratory distress; (2) ระวัง LV dysfunction → milrinone อาจ help กว่า iNO alone; (3) T21 overrepresented ใน ECMO registry + survival ↓; consult ECMO team early. อย่า underestimate PPHN ใน T21 เพราะคิดว่า 'Down syndrome แค่นั้นเอง'.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Abman SH et al Circulation 2015;132:2037 (AHA/ATS Guidelines): comprehensive pediatric PH guidelines including PPHN management; 8 PPHN-specific recommendations Class I–IIb
Steinhorn RH Fanaroff Ch73 (2025): comprehensive review of pulmonary vascular development + PPHN management; basis of this lesson
Semenza GL Annu Rev Pathol 2014;9:47: HIF-1α as master regulator of hypoxic response; role in angiogenesis, erythropoiesis, glycolytic metabolism
Bhatt AJ et al Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2001;164:1971: disrupted pulmonary vasculature + ↓ VEGF/Flt-1/TIE-2 in human infants dying with BPD — VEGF essential for lung vascular maintenance
Masarwa R et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2019;220:57 (Meta-analysis): 11 studies, 156,978 pregnancies; SSRIs → 2x ↑ PPHN risk; sertraline lowest probability risk
Van Marter LJ et al Pediatrics 2013;131:79: large multicenter study; non-aspirin NSAIDs + ibuprofen 3rd trimester: NO significant association with PPHN risk
Pels A et al Eur J Pediatr 2022;181:1709 (STRIDER trial Dutch cohort): maternal sildenafil for severe early IUGR → 12% PPHN in sildenafil arm → trial SUSPENDED
Byers HM et al Pediatr Res 2012;71:162: CRHR1 genetic variants + ↑ 17-hydroxyprogesterone associated with PPHN; no differences in adult PAH candidate genes
Galambos C et al Eur Respir J 2019;54:1801965: TBX4 mutations in neonatal + pediatric PH phenotype characterization
Bush DS & Ivy DD Cardiol Ther 2022;11:33: review PH in Down syndrome population; 10–100x higher incidence
Smith A et al J Pediatr 2022;245:172: LV diastolic dysfunction in T21 neonates independently associated with PH measures in first week
Stankiewicz P et al Am J Hum Genet 2009;84:780: FOXF1 mutations + genomic deletions in ACD; established FOXF1 as disease gene
Lakshminrusimha S et al Pediatr Res 2009;66:539: O2 + hemodynamics in newborn lambs with PH; vasodilatory plateau at ~60% O2 / PaO2 60 mmHg — hyperoxia beyond this point not beneficial
Mourani PM et al Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2015;191:87: early PVD in preterm at risk for BPD; echo findings at 7 days (IVS flattening + RV dilation in 40%) predicted BPD + late PH
Pierce CM et al J Pediatr 2021;237:154 (RCT): multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled; IV sildenafil → NO benefit on treatment failure or time on iNO in PPHN
Steinhorn RH et al J Pediatr 2016;177:90 (FUTURE-4): multicenter placebo-controlled; bosentan adjunctive to iNO → NO improvement in PPHN outcomes, time on iNO, time to extubation
Kashyap AJ et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020;105:18: physiologically based cord clamping in CDH lambs → ↑ cardiopulmonary hemodynamics + enhanced PVR drop postnatally
Mahgoub L et al Pediatr Pulmonol 2017;52:1063: pulmonary vein stenosis in ex-preterm with PH-BPD; multicenter; mortality 30–50% in first 2 years; median diagnosis 6 months
Rosenberg AA et al J Perinatol 2010;30:127: school-age outcomes PPHN cohort; 9% severe intellectual disability; 7% moderate intellectual disability
Steurer MA et al Pediatrics 2017;139:e20161165: late preterm + term PPHN in California; mortality 7.6% in modern era (iNO + ECMO)
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: อธิบาย NO-cGMP pathway ใน pulmonary vascular SMC และ ยาใดที่กระทำผ่าน pathway นี้

ตอบ: NO (endogenous หรือ inhaled) → สร้าง cGMP จาก GTP โดย soluble guanylate cyclase → cGMP ↓ myosin light chain phosphorylation → SMC relaxation. cGMP ถูก degrade โดย PDE5 (cGMP-specific PDE). ยา: (1) iNO: ให้ NO โดยตรง (selective — ventilated lung only); (2) Sildenafil: PDE5 inhibitor → ↓ cGMP breakdown → ↑ cGMP. PGI2-cAMP pathway แยกต่างหาก: PGI2 → adenylate cyclase → ↑ cAMP → SMC relax; PDE3 degrades cAMP; milrinone = PDE3 inhibitor.
Q: PPHN มี 3 categories อะไรบ้าง และ CDH จัดอยู่ใน category ใด?

ตอบ: (1) Parenchymal disease (80–90%): pulmonary vasoconstriction จาก ↓ alveolar O2; MAS/RDS/pneumonia/sepsis — almost always มี some vascular remodeling. (2) Idiopathic/remodeled (10–20%): normal parenchyma + antenatal vascular remodeling; SM hyperplasia to intra-acinar level; SSRI-exposed; antenatal ductal constriction. (3) Hypoplastic vasculature: ↓ cross-sectional vascular area + structural remodeling + altered reactivity + LV dysfunction → CDH อยู่ใน category นี้ — most common ECMO indication in neonates.
Q: ทำไม alkalosis ใน PPHN ถึงเป็น obsolete practice และ risk คืออะไร?

ตอบ: เดิมใช้ก่อนยุค iNO: hyperventilation หรือ NaHCO3 ↑ pH → transient PaO2 ↑. แต่: prolonged alkalosis (1) ไม่ improve survival; (2) worsen PVR/reactivity/permeability edema; (3) cerebral vasoconstriction → ↓ CBF → worse NDD outcomes. Current target: pH >7.3 (correct acidosis) แต่ avoid alkalosis. Acidosis เป็น pulmonary vasoconstrictor เฉพาะเมื่อรวมกับ hypoxia.
Q: ACD: genetic basis + prognosis + วิธี confirm diagnosis คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Genetic: FOXF1 mutations/deletions ใน 40%+ (Stankiewicz 2009; Jourdan-Voyen 2020); >70 FOXF1 variants + >60 CNV deletions. 10% familial. Prognosis: generally fatal even with ECMO; limited survival after lung transplant. Histology required: (1) arterioles + misaligned PVs in same adventitial sheath; (2) alveolar simplification; (3) bronchopulmonary anastomoses (so-called 'misaligned veins'). ถ้า genetic analysis ล่าช้า → timely lung biopsy; complete postmortem evaluation for all unexplained/prolonged PH deaths.
Q: ทำไม iNO ไม่ใช้ใน TAPVR และ echo findings ใดที่ต้อง rule out ก่อน start iNO?

ตอบ: TAPVR: pulmonary veins drain to right heart (obstructed หรือ unobstructed). iNO → ↑ pulmonary blood flow → เพิ่ม volume ใน obstructed pulmonary venous system → flash pulmonary edema + cardiovascular collapse. Echo ก่อน iNO: (1) confirm R→L shunting ที่ FO/DA (= true PPHN pattern); (2) rule out TAPVR: visualize pulmonary veins entering left atrium; (3) assess LV size + function (LV dysfunction → first use milrinone, not iNO); (4) IVS position, TR velocity, RV size.
Q: Down syndrome + PPHN: ทำไม LV diastolic dysfunction สำคัญและ management approach อย่างไร?

ตอบ: T21: 10–100x ↑ PPHN incidence; 5.2% ใน T21 (Dutch cohort). LV diastolic impairment (Smith J Pediatr 2022): ↑ LA pressure → ↑ pulmonary venous pressure → PH from venous side (not just arterial vasoconstriction). iNO alone อาจไม่พอ ถ้า component เป็น PVH. Management: echo ประเมิน LV diastolic function (E/e' ratio, mitral inflow); milrinone ถ้า LV dysfunction; consider lower threshold for ECMO consult (↓ survival in T21).
Q: BPD-PH: incidence, mechanism, และ pulmonary vein stenosis (PVS) คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Incidence: 16–25% ใน BPD; สูงถึง 40% ใน severe BPD. Mechanism: ↓ angiogenesis + vascular pruning + ↓ alveolar-capillary surface area + ↑ vasoconstrictor responses to hypoxia. PVS: complication ของ severe BPD; left-sided veins (mostly left upper) affected most; median diagnosis 6 months; progressive; mortality 30–50% ใน 2 ปี. Postnatally acquired (no twin concordance, NEC association). Management of BPD-PH: treat underlying BPD + hypoxia; oral sildenafil/treprostinil for chronic PH; PVS may need interventional catheter or surgical repair.
Q: Hyperoxia ใน PPHN: benefits vs risks และ target PaO2 คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Benefit: O2 เป็น pulmonary vasodilator + ↑ O2 delivery ไป brain/other tissues. Risk: plateau ที่ ~60% FiO2 / PaO2 ~60 mmHg; เกินนั้น: ↑ oxidant stress + ↑ pulmonary vascular contractility + ↓ response to NO + systemic toxicity. Target: PaO2 60–80 mmHg (AHA/ATS). ใน therapeutic hypothermia: ODC shifts left → PaO2 สูงขึ้น ณ saturation เดิม → correct blood gas to actual temp ก่อน interpret. 100% FiO2 ตลอด ไม่ใช่ goal.
Q: iNO RCTs: iNO ลด ECMO หรือไม่? ลด mortality หรือไม่? และ optimal OI window คืออะไร?

ตอบ: iNO ลด need for ECMO (Class I, LOE A) — primary benefit. iNO ไม่ลด mortality หรือ LOS ใน RCT ใดเลย. Optimal OI: >25 = threshold for start; OI 15–25 (early use) = ไม่ improve outcomes; OI >40 = ECMO threshold; delaying until OI >40 อาจ ↑ time on O2 (Gonzalez J Perinatol 2010). Rebound PH: wean to 1 ppm before stopping. Dose: 20 ppm = standard; >20 ppm: no benefit + ↑ methemoglobin risk.

Day 171 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.74
บทต่อไป: Chapter 74 — Diagnosis and Management of Congenital Heart Disease. เตรียมตัว: approach to cyanotic CHD in NICU; duct-dependent lesions (systemic vs pulmonary circulation); prostaglandin E1 dosing + side effects; echocardiographic diagnosis approach; hypoplastic left heart syndrome, transposition of great arteries, tetralogy of Fallot, truncus arteriosus, total anomalous pulmonary venous return; surgical timing + referral; how to distinguish PPHN from CHD at bedside; newborn cardiac screening (pulse oximetry) protocol.
